diagrams.title: Functional Dependency Definition
diagrams.description: A visual representation showing a table with attributes on the left and a dependency arrow indicating that attribute(s) X uniquely determine attribute(s) Y. Shows an example relation with StudentID → StudentName, StudentID → Age to illustrate the concept.
elements: Relation schema box with attribute list
Dependency arrow from X to Y
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental definition of functional dependency: if two tuples agree on attributes X, they must agree on attributes Y

diagrams.title: Armstrong's Axioms
diagrams.description: Three inference rules displayed as mathematical equations with visual representation: Reflexivity (Y ⊆ X → X → Y), Augmentation (X → Y → XZ → YZ), and Transitivity (X → Y and Y → Z → X → Z). Each axiom shown with a Venn diagram relationship.
elements: Venn diagrams showing attribute set relationships
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the three sound and complete inference rules used to derive functional dependencies closure

diagrams.title: Attribute Closure Computation
diagrams.description: Shows step-by-step algorithm for computing attribute closure F+. Starting with an initial attribute set, iteratively adding attributes that are determined by FDs. Uses a flowchart-style progression showing the iterative process with a sample set of FDs.
elements: Iteration steps showing which FD applies
diagrams.purpose: Teaches how to compute the closure of attributes under a set of functional dependencies, a key algorithm for normalization
